ACL|2021|a maximum entropy approach to n.note: Computational Linguistics 1996 (J96-1002). The printed journal header lists 'now at' addresses (Berger=Columbia University, Vincent J. Della Pietra=Renaissance Technologies), but the first-page footnote states the research was conducted while all authors were at the IBM T.J. Watson Research Center. Credited to IBM as the institution that produced the honored work (same principle as crediting work-location over later moves). JUDGMENT CALL - flagged for review.

ACL|2021|assessing agreement on classifi.note: Single author; 'University of Edinburgh' printed under the name on the 1996 Computational Linguistics PDF (J96-2004).

ACL|2021|finding deceptive opinion spam.note: ACL 2011 (P11-1032). All four authors at Cornell University, Ithaca NY; Ott/Choi/Cardie in Computer Science, Hancock in Communication.

ACL|2022|open language learning for info.note: EMNLP-CoNLL 2012 (D12-1048). All authors at Turing Center, Computer Science & Engineering, University of Washington, Seattle.

ACL|2023|universal dependency annotation.note: ACL 2013 (P13-2017). Affiliation footnotes: McDonald (first) = Google; last author Jungmee Lee = Appen-Butler-Hill (a language-services company). Country US for the Butler Hill operating entity (parent Appen is AU).

ACL|2024|glove global vectors for word r.note: EMNLP 2014 (D14-1162). All three authors at Computer Science Department, Stanford University.

ACL|2024|measures of distributional simi.note: Single author; ACL 1999 (P99-1004). Department of Computer Science, Cornell University.

ACL|2025|automatic labeling of semantic.note: ACL 2000 (P00-1065). Gildea (first) dual = UC Berkeley + International Computer Science Institute; Jurafsky (last) = University of Colorado Boulder. Different institutions.

ACL|2025|weakly supervised learning of s.note: TACL 2013 (Q13-1005). Both authors at Computer Science & Engineering, University of Washington, Seattle.

CVPR|2022|are we ready for autonomous dr.note: CVPR 2012. Geiger & Lenz = Karlsruhe Institute of Technology; Urtasun (last) = Toyota Technological Institute at Chicago (rurtasun@ttic.edu).

CVPR|2026|deep residual learning for ima.note: CVPR 2016 (ResNet) / arXiv 1512.03385. All four authors printed 'Microsoft Research' (work done at Microsoft Research Asia, Beijing). id man:microsoft kept country US for aggregation consistency.

ICLR|2025|neural machine translation by.note: ICLR 2015 / arXiv 1409.0473. First author Bahdanau printed under 'Jacobs University Bremen, Germany' (differs from senior authors); Cho and Bengio under Université de Montréal.

ICML|2022|building high level features u.note: ICML 2012 / arXiv 1112.6209 ('cat neuron' paper). The paper prints Stanford emails for Le (first) and Ng (last); the other six authors use google.com. This is the Google Brain project - Le was a Stanford PhD student working at Google Brain, Ng a Stanford professor who co-led Google Brain - so both credited dual Stanford + Google. JUDGMENT CALL - drop Google if strict as-printed only.
